verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Again, if you wish to be a catholic, I pray you, neither believe, nor say, nor teach that “the sacrifice of Christians ought to be offered in behalf of those who have departed out of the body without having been baptized.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Because you fail to show that the sacrifice of the Jews, which you have quoted out of the books of the Maccabees, was offered in behalf of any who had departed this life without circumcision.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: In this novel opinion of yours, which you have advanced against the authority and teaching of the whole Church, you have used a very arrogant mode of expression.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: You say, “In behalf of these, I most certainly decide that constant oblations and incessant sacrifices must be offered up on the part of the holy priests.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Here you show, as a layman, no submission to God’s priests for instruction; nor do you associate yourself with them (the least you could do) for inquiry; but you put yourself before them by your proud assumption of judgment.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Away, my son, with all this pretension; men walk not so arrogantly in the Way, which the Humble Christ taught that He Himself is.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: No man enters through His narrow gate with so proud a disposition as this.
